description: Population frequency summary: inverted-allele frequency, class counts, HWE test, family linkage, polymorphism class. Gated by SUPPORTED validation because the numbers are only meaningful if the group assignments are trustworthy. Distinct from internal_dynamics — this block is about the POPULATION, that block is about WITHIN-sample mosaicism. v3 RECONCILIATION: merges v1 documentation (expected_counts_hwe, note_on_hatchery_hwe, richer descriptions, explicit HWE method notes) with v2 semantics (5-value family_linkage axis, jackknife_max_delta/n_contributing, polymorphism_class). Also fixes three bugs: (1) family_linkage enum now includes 'uncertain' which C01f emits at line 553; (2) jackknife_n_contributing added to keys_extracted (was declared as property but not extracted in v2); (3) freq_class widened to 5-class to match characterize_candidate.R's derivation (previous 4-class enum would have rejected 'intermediate' and 'nearly_fixed'). Written first by STEP_C01i_d_seal (partial: freq + counts + HWE + placeholder family_linkage), then updated by STEP_C01f_hypothesis_tests (family_linkage from jackknife + derived polymorphism_class).
question: Q6

freq_inv.description: Inverted-allele frequency = (2*n_HOM_INV + n_HET) / (2*n_total). Recombinants excluded from denominator; they are neither HOM_REF nor HOM_INV alleles in the strict sense.

freq_class.description: Derived from freq_inv. Thresholds: rare <5%, low 5-15%, intermediate 15-50%, high 50-85%, nearly_fixed >=85%. Matches characterize_candidate.R's classification thresholds.

n_total.description: Count of classified samples (HOM_REF + HET + HOM_INV + RECOMBINANT).

class_counts.description: Per-class sample counts. In v3, these are also emitted as flat keys (q6_n_HOM_REF, q6_n_HET, q6_n_HOM_INV, q6_n_Recombinant) so consumers can read without parsing the nested object.

expected_counts_hwe.description: Expected HOM_REF / HET / HOM_INV counts under HWE at observed freq_inv. Useful for supplementary tables showing observed-vs-expected without reviewers having to recompute.

hwe_method.description: HWE test method. v3 default is chi_squared (2 df). exact reserved for future use with small n or extreme frequencies.

hwe_chi2.description: Chi-squared statistic (2 df) from observed vs expected-under-HWE class counts.

hwe_verdict.description: consistent if p >= 0.05; het_excess / het_deficit if p < 0.05 based on sign of (observed_het - expected_het); underpowered when n_total < 30 or any expected cell < 5.

genotype_balance.description: Classification axis for downstream, mirrors hwe_verdict but without the underpowered case — forces a direction call.

family_linkage.description: Six-class axis derived from cheat6 jackknife verdict in C01f: multi_family = robust, segregates in many families (robust_multi_family or multi_family_contributing); few_family = 2-3 families drive the signal (few_family_contributing); single_family = family-specific polymorphism, real but restricted (single_family_fragile); pca_family_confounded = PCA groups track ancestry not arrangement (the SUSPECT case, fragile or pca_family_driven); uncertain = verdict missing but jackknife delta high (fallback branch); unknown = jackknife not run or inconclusive (seal placeholder before C01f overwrites). FIX in v3: 'uncertain' was emitted by C01f at L553 but missing from v2 enum; added here.

jackknife_max_delta.description: Max |delta| across ancestry-group jackknife iterations. Sister field to q7_t9_max_delta. Populated by C01f after T9 runs.

jackknife_n_contributing.description: Number of ancestry groups whose removal drops the inversion signal (delta > threshold). FIX in v3: added to keys_extracted — was declared as property but never extracted as a flat key in v2.

polymorphism_class.description: Derived from family_linkage: multi_family -> cohort_wide; few_family -> lineage_restricted; single_family -> family_restricted; pca_family_confounded / uncertain / unknown -> unclassified. v3 NOTE: derivation is performed in utils/registry_key_helpers.R::update_C01f_frequency_linkage (helper-side), not in STEP_C01f_hypothesis_tests.R source. This keeps C01f untouched while still producing the derived value at block-write time.

computed_from.description: Provenance: which writer last touched this block. C01i_d_seal_initial = just written by seal with placeholder family_linkage; C01f_updated = C01f has overwritten family_linkage + polymorphism_class; C01i_d_seal_only = seal ran but C01f has not.

note_on_hatchery_hwe.description: F1 hybrid hatchery samples systematically violate HWE at neutral loci due to population admixture. A het_excess is EXPECTED; het_deficit is the more informative deviation (suggests assortative mating or selection against heterozygotes).

note_on_reconciliation: v3 = v1 documentation (expected_counts_hwe, note_on_hatchery_hwe, richer HWE descriptions) + v2 semantics (5-value family_linkage, jackknife fields, polymorphism_class) + three bug fixes (uncertain in enum, jackknife_n_contributing extracted, freq_class 5-class to match consumer). v1 and v2 archived to _archive_superseded/bk_schemas_pre_canonical/. The helper-side derivation of polymorphism_class is documented on the field itself and is NOT a writer modification — C01f source is unchanged.
